rank.description: PG ts_rank score on the (title A, body B) tsvector. Higher = more relevant. Returned even when order='recent' so clients can render a relevance badge.

data.description: Up to `limit` (default 20, max 50) hits. Not paginated in v1.

pagination.description: Always `null` — this lane returns a single bounded, ranked page (no cursor in v1). Present for envelope consistency.

allow_quote.description: When false, quoting this post (kind='quote') is refused (422 QUOTE_NOT_ALLOWED).

allow_repost.description: When false, reposting this post is refused (422 REPOST_NOT_ALLOWED).

comments.description: Who may comment. `everyone` (default), `followers`, `mentioned` (actors mentioned in the post body), or `off` (author only). Enforced server-side on comment create.

hide_reaction_counts.description: When true, `reaction_counts` is suppressed (emitted as `{}`) for everyone except the post author, on every read surface.

allow_quote.description: Allow this post to be quoted. Default true.

allow_repost.description: Allow this post to be reposted. Default true.

hide_reaction_counts.description: Suppress reaction_counts for everyone except the author. Default false.

post_ids.description: UUIDs of the posts to hydrate viewer state for. Capped at 200 (matches the batch-views precedent); ids that are unknown, removed, or from another community resolve to the empty state instead of failing the batch.

data.description: One entry per requested post id (de-duplicated, request order). Unknown / cross-community ids resolve to the empty state.

created_at.description: ISO timestamp the tag was created.

updated_at.description: ISO timestamp of the last state change.

bookmarked.description: The viewer has bookmarked the post.

reacted_types.description: Reaction types the viewer currently has on the post, sorted ascending. Empty when the viewer has not reacted.

reposted.description: The viewer has a currently-published repost of the post.

voted_option.description: Poll option index the viewer voted for; `null` when the viewer has not voted (or the post has no poll).

reaction_counts.description: Denormalised reaction counts after the write.

created_at.description: ISO timestamp.

dst_actor_id.description: Destination actor UUID — always `null` for post reactions (the target is `dst_post_id`).

created.description: `true` on first call; `false` on idempotent retry.

reaction_counts.description: Denormalised reaction counts after the write.

actor_id.description: Viewer social actor UUID. Standardized name (task 008 / G11).

post_ids.description: UUIDs of posts that became visible to the viewer in this batch. Capped at 200 to keep the payload sensible — the server will silently skip ids that aren't in the community or have been removed.

viewer_actor_id.description: Optional viewer actor UUID (task 021). When present, the impressions count toward the post's unique reach (deduped per viewer/day); omit for anonymous views (which count total views but not reach).

blocked_by.description: This actor has blocked the source actor — surface it BEFORE attempting a follow/react write instead of handling the 409.

blocking.description: The source actor has blocked this actor.

close_friend.description: This actor is on the source actor's close-friend list.

dst_actor_id.description: Destination actor UUID this entry describes.

follow_pending.description: The source actor has a pending follow request against this (private) actor (task 014).

followed_by.description: This actor follows the source actor.

following.description: The source actor follows this actor.

muting.description: The source actor has muted this actor.

restricting.description: The source actor has restricted this actor.

data.description: One entry per requested id (de-duplicated, request order). Unknown ids resolve to all-false flags.

actor_id.description: DEPRECATED — use `?actor_id=`. Caller social actor UUID (must equal the list owner).
DEPRECATED — use `?actor_id=`. Acting actor UUID (equal to the target for self-leave).

caller_actor_id.description: Social actor UUID of the renaming admin.

name.description: New display name. Pass null or empty to clear.

data.description: Page of repost post rows, each carrying the reposting actor_id.

actor_id.description: The sender — social actor UUID. Standardized name (task 008 / G11); the persisted `sender_id` defaults from it.

assets.description: Ordered image attachments (task 019). Each references a `ready` asset in this community owned by the sender. Max 20.

body.description: Message body. Required when `kind="text"`. 1..10000 chars after trim.

actor_id.description: Author actor UUID.

has_unviewed.description: True when the requester has not viewed the latest story.

latest_story_created_at.description: When the most recent unexpired-or-pinned story was created.

story_count.description: Total unexpired-or-pinned stories from this author.

pagination.description: Always `null` — the tray is a single bounded page (≤200 entries, no cursor in v1). The key is present for envelope consistency with cursor-paginated lists (task 008 / G13).

reason.description: Why this candidate surfaced. `followed_by_friends` is the friend-of-a-friend signal; `top_in_community` is the cold-start fallback used when FoF doesn't fill the requested limit.

score.description: For `followed_by_friends`: number of the requester's followees who follow this candidate. For `top_in_community`: the candidate's `follower_count`.

data.description: Ordered candidates. FoF candidates are returned first (highest count first); cold-start fallbacks fill any remaining slots up to the requested limit.

pagination.description: Always `null` — suggestions are a single bounded, scored page (no cursor in v1). Present for envelope consistency (task 008 / G13).

count.description: Number of unread notifications for the actor.
Number of unread incoming messages across every conversation the actor participates in.

is_private.description: Make the actor private (task 014). Caps effective post visibility at `followers`; following requires an approved request. Flipping back to public auto-approves pending requests.

caller_actor_id.description: Caller — social actor UUID. Must equal the list owner.
The caller — must be the owner.

status.description: Direct status transition. Prefer the moderation lane for `hidden`/`removed`.

settings.description: Replaces the settings blob. Supported keys are typed on CommunitySettingsDto; unrecognized keys are stored verbatim but not consumed.

actor_id.description: The participant whose row is being updated.

muted.description: Mute / unmute on the caller's participant row.

caller_actor_id.description: The admin performing the role change — social actor UUID.

actor_id.description: The sender — must match the message's sender_id. Standardized name (task 008 / G11).

read.description: `true` to mark read, `false` to mark unread.

enabled.description: `true` to receive notifications of this kind, `false` to mute. Default behaviour (no row) is `true`.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 expiry timestamp; pass null to clear.

pinned.description: Toggle pinned status (used by highlights / stories).

scheduled_for.description: ISO-8601 publish-at timestamp. Updating only valid while the post is `status="scheduled"`. Pass null to clear (typically only the scheduler does this on promotion).

settings.description: Partial update of the per-post interaction settings. Only the keys you pass change; the rest keep their current value.

status.description: Direct status transition. Pass `published` to promote a `draft`/`scheduled` or restore an `archived` post; pass `archived` to hide-without-deleting (engagement kept). Prefer the moderation lane for `hidden`/`removed`.

tags.description: Additional actor UUIDs to tag (task 011). Additive — creates new pending tags; never resets existing approved/removed tags. Max 30.

disabled.description: Set `false` to re-enable a disabled webhook (clears the auto-disable state and resets the failure streak — no secret rotation required). Set `true` to disable it manually.

event_types.description: New subscription set. Unknown values are rejected with a 400 naming the valid set.

display_name.description: Display name shown in feeds / mentions.

external_id.description: Customer-side identifier (your user id). Idempotency key for upsert; unique per community.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata blob (e.g. plan tier, internal flags).

created.description: `true` on first call (row was inserted), `false` on subsequent upserts that updated an existing row.

data.description: Every event type a webhook can subscribe to, in catalog order.
Every webhook on the community — disabled rows included, so auto-disabled subscribers stay discoverable.

consecutive_failures.description: Consecutive failed attempts since the last success.

created_at.description: ISO timestamp.

disabled_at.description: ISO timestamp when the webhook was auto-disabled (or null if active).

disabled_reason.description: Reason the dispatcher auto-disabled the row.

event_types.description: Subscribed event types. Each item is one of the social event names — see GET /v1/webhook-event-types for the live catalog.

last_failure_at.description: ISO timestamp of the most recent non-2xx response.

last_success_at.description: ISO timestamp of the most recent 2xx response.

signing_secret_hint.description: Last 4 chars of the signing secret. Use the full secret returned by create / rotate to verify the X-Pcft-Signature header.

info.description: Social-as-a-Service backend: actors, posts, comments, edges (follow/mute/block/restrict), feeds, stories, DMs, moderation. Two lanes. Customer lane: your backend calls /v1/communities/:communityId/* with a workspace PAK (`Authorization: Bearer pcft_live_...`) and asserts the acting end-user via an actor UUID field (`actor_id` / `src_actor_id` / `requester_id` depending on the route). Admin lane: /v1/workspaces/:workspaceId/* accepts a PAK bearer, a PlatformUser bearer, or the `auth_token` session cookie, and covers community CRUD, moderation, and analytics.

parameters.description: Opaque client-generated string (1–256 chars, `[A-Za-z0-9_\-:]`) used to deduplicate retries within a 24h window. The signing secret is returned exactly once; a replay returns the original response (including the secret) with `Idempotent-Replay: true`. Same key + different body returns 409 `IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSE`.
parameters.name: Idempotency-Key

400.description: Validation failure (bad url, empty or unknown event_types — the message names the valid set; malformed Idempotency-Key).

409.description: Webhook cap reached (10 per community), or `IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSE` (same Idempotency-Key, different body).

post.summary: Create a community webhook. Multiple subscribers per community are supported (cap 10). Returns the signing secret ONCE — store it. Pass an `Idempotency-Key` header so a retried create replays the SAME secret instead of minting a duplicate subscriber.

delete.summary: Delete the webhook.

get.summary: Read a single webhook by id.

patch.summary: Update webhook url, subscribed event types, or disabled state. `{ "disabled": false }` is the re-enable path after an auto-disable — no secret rotation required.

get.summary: List delivery attempts (newest first).

409.description: Webhook is disabled — re-enable it first.

post.summary: Re-publish a past delivery from its stored request body. Async (202). The payload is byte-identical to the original (same event id), so subscriber-side event-id dedupe treats it as a redelivery.

parameters.description: Opaque client-generated string (1–256 chars, `[A-Za-z0-9_\-:]`) used to deduplicate retries within a 24h window. A replay returns the original response (including the new secret) with `Idempotent-Replay: true`; a retry WITHOUT the header rotates the secret again, invalidating the previous one. Same key + different body returns 409 `IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSE`.
parameters.name: Idempotency-Key

409.description: `IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSE` — the supplied Idempotency-Key was already used with a different request body.

post.summary: Rotate the signing secret. Also clears any auto-disable flag. Returns the new secret ONCE. Pass an `Idempotency-Key` header so a retried rotate replays the SAME new secret instead of rotating twice.

409.description: Webhook is disabled — re-enable it first.

post.summary: Enqueue a `ping` test delivery. Async (202): the dispatch worker signs and POSTs it exactly like a real event — SSRF guard, TLS, HMAC signature and all. Watch the deliveries list for the outcome.

get.summary: List the webhook event-type catalog. Static metadata, no auth required. Create/update webhooks reject anything not in this list with a 400.
